nodes.description: Surgical removal of shoot apex releasing axillary buds
Shade signal; degrades FHY3 and phyB
High N upregulates IPT-mediated CK biosynthesis, represses SL
Day length signal acting via CO-FT pathway
Primary carbon/signaling metabolite
Age-regulated microRNA silencing SPL9
Constitutive F-box protein; SL and karrikin perception gate co-inhibitor
Short-chain alcohol dehydrogenase in ABA biosynthesis
Carotenoid isomerase; first committed step of SL biosynthesis
Cytochrome P450 oxidizing carlactone in SL biosynthesis; repressed by GA (iter 3 cross-hormone)
2-OGD oxidoreductase; final SL biosynthesis step
Near-source hormone maintaining apical dominance and promoting SL biosynthesis
Carotenoid cleavage enzyme in SL biosynthesis
Carotenoid cleavage enzyme in SL biosynthesis
Branching-inhibiting hormone; Perception Gate via D14+MAX2 with Trap 5 CKX9 branch
SL receptor, co-inhibitor of SMXL678 with MAX2; self-degradation (Motif 6)
SL-regulated repressors; hub repressing BRC1/SPL9, promoting PIN1
MAX2-degraded TF; BRC1 co-repressor (Motif 5 multi-output)
Activated by Auxin, balanced by GA2OX catabolism (Motif 4). Degrades DELLA.
GA-inactivating enzyme; induced by GA (negative feedback Motif 2)
GA-degraded repressors; sequester SPL9
BRC1 activator; repressed by miR156/DELLA/SMXL678/FHY3 (all-inhibitor node)
Master branching repressor TF; integrator of 3 activators and 7 inhibitors
Rate-limiting CK biosynthesis; activated by Nitrogen and Decapitation, repressed by Auxin
CK-degrading enzyme; induced by SL (Trap 5 framework limitation documented)
Bud outgrowth hormone; balanced by IPT (synthesis) and CKX9 (degradation)
Auxin efflux carrier; polarization required for bud outgrowth canalization
Auxin efflux carrier; connective transport between buds
Transposase-derived TF; promotes SMXL678, inhibits SPL9 (Motif 3); shade-degraded
Sugar signaling metabolite; reflects Sucrose status
Florigen; promotes flowering and represses BRC1
HD-Zip I TF (representing HB21/40/53); activates NCED3 downstream of BRC1
Rate-limiting ABA biosynthesis enzyme
Dormancy hormone downstream of BRC1-HB21-NCED3 cascade
BRC1-inhibiting co-repressor; activated by Cytokinin via ARR1 (iter 3: no longer source)
B-type response regulator; direct CK target activating TIE1 (Yang 2022)
Auxin-induced malectin-type receptor-like kinase; phosphorylates PIN1 for polar localization (Hajny 2020)
Number of axillary branches; integrative phenotype output

edges.mechanism: BRC1-dependent activation of ABI1 (PP2C ABA receptor partner) in dormant buds; among 26 ABA-related BRC1-dependent genes

evidence.title: Identification of gene functions associated to active and dormant buds in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: ABA, including ABI1, ABF3, AFP1, AFP3, NAC019, NAC092, and NAP
evidence.claim: BRC1 activates ABI1 in dormant buds

edges.mechanism: Auxin upregulates MAX3/CCD7 expression in AXR1-dependent manner

evidence.evidence_sentence: Auxin up-regulation of MAX3 and MAX4 transcripts in an AXR1-dependent manner

evidence.title: Strigolactone Acts Downstream of Auxin to Regulate Bud Outgrowth in Pea and Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Brewer PB, Dun EA, Ferguson BJ, Rameau C, Beveridge CA
evidence.evidence_sentence: Decapitation resulted in reduced IAA levels and decreased expression of SMS synthesis genes.

evidence.title: The pea branching RMS2 gene encodes the PsAFB4/5 auxin receptor and is involved in an auxin-strigolactone regulation loop

evidence.evidence_sentence: Auxin application to the decapitated stump or to intact plants results in an increase in transcript abundance of CCD7 (RMS5) and CCD8 (RMS1)
evidence.claim: Auxin induces SL biosynthesis genes CCD7/MAX3 and CCD8/MAX4

edges.mechanism: Auxin upregulates MAX4/CCD8 expression in AXR1-dependent manner

evidence.title: Interactions between Auxin and Strigolactone in Shoot Branching Control
evidence.evidence_sentence: MAX3 and MAX4 transcripts are induced by auxin in an AXR1-dependent manner
evidence.claim: Auxin promotes SL biosynthesis via CCD8

evidence.title: Strigolactone Acts Downstream of Auxin to Regulate Bud Outgrowth in Pea and Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Brewer PB, Dun EA, Ferguson BJ, Rameau C, Beveridge CA
evidence.evidence_sentence: Auxin regulation of the expression of the SMS synthesis gene CCD8 was demonstrated to be AXR1 dependent.
evidence.claim: Auxin upregulates CCD8/MAX4 (and other SL biosynthesis genes) via AXR1-dependent signaling

evidence.title: The pea branching RMS2 gene encodes the PsAFB4/5 auxin receptor and is involved in an auxin-strigolactone regulation loop
evidence.authors: Ligerot Y, de Saint Germain A, Waldie T, Troadec C, Citerne S, Kadakia N, Pillot JP, Prigge M, Aubert G, Bendahmane A, Leyser O, Estelle M, Debelle F, Rameau C

evidence.evidence_sentence: MAX4 encodes plastid-targeted carotenoid cleavage dioxygenase

evidence.title: Strigolactones and Shoot Branching: What Is the Real Hormone and How Does It Work?
evidence.authors: Dun EA, Brewer PB, Gillam EMJ, Beveridge CA

evidence.title: Unveiling the complexity of strigolactones: exploring structural diversity, biosynthesis pathways, and signaling mechanisms

evidence.title: Strigolactones and Shoot Branching: What Is the Real Hormone and How Does It Work?
evidence.authors: Dun EA, Brewer PB, Gillam EMJ, Beveridge CA
evidence.evidence_sentence: MAX1/CYP711A1; LBO (2OGDD enzyme); CLAMT.

edges.mechanism: BRC1 lowers the basal (non-polar) rate of auxin efflux from buds; in Arabidopsis BRC1 does not directly transcriptionally target PIN3 (unlike cucumber), instead acting on broader efflux machinery and via auxin conjugation through GH3.5.

evidence.title: A BRC1-modulated switch in auxin efflux accounts for the competition between Arabidopsis axillary buds

evidence.evidence_sentence: BRC1 downregulates the basal rate of auxin efflux in buds.
evidence.claim: BRC1 reduces basal auxin efflux from axillary buds

edges.mechanism: SPLAYED (SYD), a SWI/SNF chromatin-remodeling ATPase, is required for SL-mediated transcriptional induction of BRC1

evidence.authors: Humphreys JL, Beveridge CA, Tanurdzic M
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRANCHED1 (BRC1): Identified as a critical SL-induced transcription factor whose induction requires the SWI/SNF ATPase SPLAYED (SYD)
evidence.claim: SYD is necessary for SL-induced BRC1 expression
